evals.prompt: I'm building a Playwright scraper in Python that needs to log in to a site, but the login form has a reCAPTCHA v2 checkbox. The sitekey is '6Le-wvkSAAAAAPBMRTvw0Q4Muexq9bi0DJwx_mJ-' and I have a 2captcha API key in my CAPTCHA_API_KEY env var. Show me how to solve the captcha and inject the token so the form submits successfully. The login page is at https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api2/demo
evals.expected_output: Should use scripts/solve_captcha.py with --type recaptcha-v2, extract sitekey, call the solver, inject the token into [name='g-recaptcha-response'], trigger the grecaptcha callback, then submit. Should include working Python+Playwright code.
expectations: The solution uses or references scripts/solve_captcha.py rather than embedding raw API calls inline
The code reads the CAPTCHA API key from the CAPTCHA_API_KEY environment variable (not hardcoded)
The code injects the solved token into the [name='g-recaptcha-response'] hidden field
The code triggers the grecaptcha callback after injection (references ___grecaptcha_cfg or equivalent)
The solution is a complete, runnable Python script (not just pseudocode or a description)

evals.prompt: I need to write a Python script that visits https://2captcha.com/demo/recaptcha-v2, detects what type of captcha is on the page, solves it using CapMonster (I have my key as CAPTCHA_API_KEY), injects the solution, and clicks the Check button to confirm it worked. Can you write the full automation script for me?
evals.expected_output: Should detect reCAPTCHA v2 on the page, use scripts/solve_captcha.py with --service capmonster, inject the token, trigger the callback, click the check/submit button, and verify success. Should be a complete runnable script.
expectations: The script navigates to https://2captcha.com/demo/recaptcha-v2 (not a hardcoded different URL)
The script uses CapMonster as the solving service (--service capmonster or service='capmonster')
The script extracts the sitekey dynamically from the page rather than hardcoding it
The script clicks a submit/check button after injecting the token
The script uses Playwright (imports or references playwright) for browser automation

evals.prompt: I need a Python Playwright script that handles a page which sometimes shows Cloudflare Turnstile (sitekey '0x4AAAAAAABkMYinukE8nzYS') and sometimes shows hCaptcha (sitekey 'a5f74b19-9e45-40e0-b45d-47ff91b7a6c2'). The script should detect which one is present, call solve_captcha.py (which is in the same directory) to solve it, then inject the result. Also add realistic browser behavior so Cloudflare doesn't flag us after the captcha passes. Use CAPTCHA_API_KEY from env.
evals.expected_output: Should use solve_captcha.py subprocess with --type turnstile and --type hcaptcha branches respectively, inject cf-turnstile-response or h-captcha-response per type, trigger data-callback, and apply stealth (--disable-blink-features, realistic UA, random delays).
expectations: The script calls solve_captcha.py via subprocess with --type turnstile for the Turnstile branch
The script calls solve_captcha.py via subprocess with --type hcaptcha for the hCaptcha branch
The script detects which CAPTCHA type is present and routes to the correct branch (not hardcoded to one type)
The solution applies at least one stealth measure via Playwright launch args or context options (--disable-blink-features, realistic user-agent, or add_init_script patching navigator.webdriver)
The solution includes random or variable delays between browser actions (random.uniform, random.randint, or equivalent)
